patch.value: Create/copy ID allocation TOCTOU: duplicate generated id silently overwrites the other item's file; idExists misses extension type folders

Two gaps in new-item id allocation under parallel creates. (1) TOCTOU + silent overwrite: generateItemId (src/core/item/id.ts) does a check-then-use existence probe long before writeCreatedItem acquires the per-id lock (create.ts runs parent/blocked-by resolution I/O in between), and the final writeFileAtomic RENAMES OVER any existing file. If two parallel creates draw the same id (4-char base36 = 1.68M space; a 20-create parallel burst has ~1/9000 pair-collision odds, worse as the workspace fills and token space is consumed), the second create silently destroys the first item — no error, no history anomaly on the surviving file. Fix: after acquiring the per-id lock in writeCreatedItem/copy, re-check id existence and regenerate (or fail with id_collision) before writing; or write the new item with O_EXCL semantics (open wx) instead of overwrite-rename. (2) idExists only probes built-in TYPE_TO_FOLDER folders (.md/.toon), not extension-registered item-type folders, so a generated id can collide with an extension-typed item undetected — thread the resolved type_to_folder registry into generateItemId. Empirical baseline: 30 parallel creates → 30 items, 0 collisions observed (expected at this scale); this is an extreme-case correctness guard, not an observed failure.

ID allocation is collision-safe under parallel create/copy (atomic reservation or retry-on-exists); idExists covers extension type folders; stress test creating items in parallel yields zero overwrites.

## Context
Filed by the 2026-07-03 concurrency audit. Worst-blast-radius open concurrency gap: a parallel create/copy pair drawing the same generated id silently destroys one item.

## Failure modes
1. TOCTOU + silent overwrite: generateItemId (src/core/item/id.ts) probes existence long before writeCreatedItem acquires the per-id lock (create.ts does parent/blocked-by resolution I/O in between). writeFileAtomic RENAMES OVER an existing file, so the second create replaces the first — no error, and the surviving file's history stream shows no anomaly. 4-char base36 id space = 1.68M; a 20-create burst has ~1/9000 pair-collision odds, worsening as the workspace fills (1482 items today).
2. idExists misses extension-registered type folders, so collisions with extension items are even likelier.

## Approach
After acquiring the per-id lock in writeCreatedItem/copy, re-check existence and re-draw the id on collision (bounded retries); make idExists enumerate ALL type folders including extension-registered ones (schema registry knows them). Optionally open the file with wx instead of rename-over as a belt-and-braces guard.

## Definition of done
Parallel create burst (e.g. 50 concurrent) yields 50 distinct surviving items with intact histories; a forced id collision (seeded generator) re-draws instead of overwriting; extension-type folder collision covered by a spec.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Model relationship: pm-khdq (ID-allocation TOCTOU) and pm-bgcu (singleton-file RMW loss) are the same lock-free concurrent-write race class under pm-dj98
